title: Weak Acid ICE Solver
creator: Dan McCreary
subject: AP Chemistry
description: Interactive weak-acid ICE table solver with Ka algebra, approximation checks, and percent ionization gauge.

learningObjectives: Set up and solve a weak-acid ICE table using the quadratic formula.
Decide whether the small-x approximation is valid based on the ratio x/Ca.

keyQuestions: When does the 5 percent rule hold for weak acids?
How does changing Ka or concentration alter percent ionization?
commonMisconceptions: Weak acids always ionize less than five percent regardless of concentration.
Quadratic solutions are only required for strong acids.
assessmentOpportunities: Students capture the ICE table plus approximation verdict for two acid scenarios and explain the differences.
Learners describe why diluting an acid can invalidate or validate the small-x approximation.
